messages.content: how can i make this work in react? Type 'ObjectId' is not assignable to type 'Key | null | undefined'. ObjectId is from bson

in this line of code: <div className="col-6 col-md-4 col-xl-2 my-3" key={item._id}>
i have this rs error: TS2322: Type 'ObjectId' is not assignable to type 'Key | null | undefined'.  index.d.ts(100, 9): The expected type comes from property 'key' which is declared here on type 'DetailedHTMLProps<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, HTMLDivElement>

item is of type IProduct {
    _id: ObjectId,
    name: string,
    model: string,
    image: string,
    description: string,
    price: {
        single: number,
        loyalty: number,
    },
    categories: string[] | [],
    similar: IProduct[] | []
}

how can i fix this?

To fix this error, you can convert the ObjectId to a string before passing it as the key attribute. Here's how you can do it:

```javascript
<div className="col-6 col-md-4 col-xl-2 my-3" key={item._id.toString()}>
```

This should resolve the TypeScript error and allow you to use the ObjectId as the key for your React component.
thanks
You're welcome! Let me know if you have any more questions.

that didnt help, now i have this error TS2322: Type 'ObjectId | null' is not assignable to type 'ObjectId'.   Type 'null' is not assignable to type 'ObjectId'.  DetailedProduct.tsx(11, 41): The expected type comes from property 'id' which is declared here on type 'IntrinsicAttributes & { id: ObjectId; }'
but i dont want to modify interface, because ObejctId should be always specified

well i guess my usage is incorrect, because such usage crashes server. Lets try again, i will explain usage case more specificaly:

in DetailedProductPage the id is string that represents the object from mongoose database (specifically returned from _id field). i want to pass it to DetailedProduct component to render that specific product. how should i rewrite my code and what type should i specify?
still crashes the server. Lets take another approach: how can i check if id passed in <DetailedProduct id={params.id} /> is a valid mongoose _id?

that's a great way, but for some reason my server crashes with  node_modules/bson/lib/bson.mjs:111:26: ERROR: Top-level await is not available in the configured target environment ("chrome87", "edge88", "es2020", "firefox78", "safari13" + 2 overrides)

I see. This error suggests that you may need to enable top-level `await` in your environment.

The current version of Node.js (as of writing this) supports top-level `await` if you start it with the `--experimental-top-level-await` flag.

To enable top-level `await` in your Node.js environment, you can modify your project's `package.json` file to include the following line:

```
"scripts": {
  "start": "node --experimental-top-level-await app.js"
}
```

This tells Node.js to enable top-level `await` when running your `app.js` file. You can replace `app.js` with the path to your script that uses top-level `await`.

Alternatively, you can switch to using a version of Node.js that supports top-level `await` without the need for the `--experimental-top-level-await` flag.

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any further questions.

Even though you are checking for `undefined` explicitly, TypeScript still considers the `id` variable to potentially be `undefined`, because the type of the `params.id` property is a union of `string | undefined`.

One way you can fix the TypeScript error is to assign a default value to `id` if it is `undefined`, using the nullish coalescing operator `??`. Here's how you can modify your code:

```javascript

import {DetailedProduct} from "../components/Detailed
